designed: 2026-06-16 (R570 manifest round; sha256 of this manifest pinned in the R570 ledger entry BEFORE any author/extract/judge subagent runs — the R453/R499/R514/R548/R550/R561 pre-commit pattern). Owner-CHOSEN post-R569: test whether an AI can SELF-AUTHOR a TIME-AXIS game (the Zelda Ocarina-of-Time model: the SAME place existing in PAST and FUTURE, both canonical, past acts causally changing the future) that passes the gates on the EXISTING substrate with ZERO new substrate. The next leg after the dnd-quest arc (R561-R569) proved fact->story->quest->map; this leg stresses the TIME axis + multi-character cross-region causation.
lineage_note: DESIGNED by the lineage that ran the dnd-quest arc (R561-R569). Per the R469/R500 contamination bound this lineage may NOT author the fact base, render prose, re-extract, or judge; it writes this manifest + the PREMISE (the human's premise / high-level-direction role per the AI-first doctrine) + the briefs + the runbook, and orchestrates the deterministic gates + the era/region/causation scans + the negative-control removes + the render-slice selection only.

substrate_resolution_R570.the_design_question: Is 'same place, two eras' a FRAME, a BRANCH, or a canon-ORDER coordinate? This had to be RESOLVED on code before pinning (the refute-first R493/R547/R556 discipline). It is RESOLVED.
substrate_resolution_R570.verdict: ERA = a canon-ORDER coordinate on ONE world-line. Confirmed against the schema (mnemosyne-core/src/narrative.rs): a fact carries exactly two scope axes today — `branch` ('which quest-path / playthrough world', MAIN_BRANCH doc:33-39) and `frame` ('who believes it', the epistemic POV: a character / faction / the ground-truth axis, Frame doc:49-59). NEITHER is an era. The in-world TIME position (Founding-Age scenes before Withering-Age scenes) is a THIRD, orthogonal thing — the canon-order partial-order coordinate (R466 partial order; R560 'canon order is a PARTIAL order'). So the two ages are per-era SCENES ordered past < future on one world-line, NOT two frames and NOT (for the canonical spine) two branches.

mapping.in_game_time (past<future): canon-order PARTIAL-order coordinate (the in-world time axis, distinct from discourse order). Founding-Age scenes precede Withering-Age scenes in canon order.
mapping.same place: a shared Entity (the hollow / each region) + a per-era scene per age; the disclosure-surface `object` resolved by a MapLocator (R557) is the STABLE pointer ACROSS eras (the cistern vent / the boundary-stone / the crossing-tree are the same object touched in both ages).
mapping.past->future causation: R442 setup(`Expected`, a Founding-Age fact) / payoff(a Withering-Age fact) ACROSS time-distance, PLUS R547 world-state `exclusive` succession (a Founding state -> a Withering state per object via supersedes_in_frame + holds_at). payoff_coverage is world-scoped and frame-agnostic ('Payoff edges cross FRAMES freely … but never cross worlds', continuity.rs:2442-2444) — exactly the mechanism a cross-era / cross-region / cross-frame consequence needs.
mapping.time-toggle (the wend-pipe): PLAY navigation over the frozen substrate (R560): the store statically holds BOTH ages; the wend-pipe toggle is a pointer move, NOT execution.
mapping.optional past action (plant-or-not): a fork (Branch) -> two Withering futures. OoT MIXES canonical causation (a single forward chain) with optional acts (a choice); this premise has ONE optional fork on an otherwise-canonical spine.
mapping.multiple characters: the `frame` axis (R541 proven — 12 individuated person-frames). Each character's POV = a frame.
mapping.different regions: distinct scenes / distinct MapLocator `object`s on ONE shared world-line (a region is a place, not a scope axis).
mapping.cross-region causation: `pays_off` crossing frames freely (R442 code) + R547 succession across regions: one region's / character's act reaches another's on the SAME world-line, gate-verifiable, never crossing worlds.

substrate_resolution_R570.conclusion: ZERO new substrate (the R547/R556 conclusion shape). The hypothesis is CONFIRMED on code: era, same-place, cross-era causation, cross-region/cross-frame causation, the time-toggle, and the optional fork are ALL expressible with the existing axes (order coordinate + frame + branch + R442 payoff + R547 succession + R557 MapLocator). This experiment is the falsification test of that conclusion ON SELF-AUTHORED DATA — a blind author who does not know the mapping must produce a base on which the pins below hold.

question.primary: Can an AI SELF-AUTHOR a real time-travel game — the SAME place in a PAST and a FUTURE age, both canon, with early acts whose consequences land in the late age (including ACROSS regions and ACROSS characters) — as a gate-clean fact base on the EXISTING substrate, such that (a) the two ages are an ORDER coordinate not a branch, (b) past->future causation is STRUCTURAL (gate-enforced, not prose-only), (c) a multi-character cross-region cause-effect chain is structural, and (d) it READS as a coherent time-travel game to blind judges?
question.sub_question_1_era_as_order: Does a blind author, told to write the same place in two ages with early acts changing the late age (but NOT told 'era = an order coordinate'), produce a base where the two ages sit as per-era scenes on ONE world-line ordered past<future — rather than collapsing them into two frames or two unrelated branches?
question.sub_question_2_causation_is_structural: Are the past->future links REAL in the data — a Founding setup paid off by a Withering fact (R442 across time-distance) and a Founding state superseding into a Withering state (R547) — such that REMOVING the past setup makes the future payoff surface as dangling (the causal link is gate-enforced, not just asserted in prose)?
question.sub_question_3_cross_region_cross_charac: Does the load-bearing chain hold — a setup by character A in region X (Founding) paid off by a fact by character B in region Y (Withering), cross-era AND cross-region AND cross-frame — gate-clean, and falsifiable by the same remove-and-rescan negative control?
question.sub_question_4_reads_as_time_travel: Does the rendered slice + the cross-age briefing READ to blind judges as a coherent time-travel game: the two ages consistent, the early-act/late-consequence satisfying, and the distant-places-line-up-in-time coherent?
question.isolation: the structural questions (1-3) are AUTHORING properties measured deterministically on the author's base (PIN-1..PIN-5); 'reads as time travel' (4) is the open judged question on the rendered slice + briefing. The R518 separable-question lesson.

premise.title: The Wend-Pipe of Brae Hollow (FRESH, original — no existing IP)
premise.fresh_rationale: A two-age hollow built to DEMAND the time axis: one place (Brae Hollow) in a Founding Age and a Withering Age; three regions present in both ages (Cistern / Loom Quarter / Wend Fields); a stable prop per region across ages (cistern vent / boundary-stone / crossing-tree); a canonical past->future spine (Sera seals the cistern -> the late cistern runs clean) PLUS the load-bearing CROSS-REGION CROSS-CHARACTER chain (Halden marks the ford in the Loom Quarter -> Ode crosses safely in the Wend Fields, three generations on); ONE optional Founding act (plant the gorge-sapling) -> two Withering futures; and a withheld Founding-Age CAUSE the late age cannot see until the bearer travels. Time-axis-native by construction; NO convergence required (the R527/R537 axis is orthogonal).
premise.human_role_note: Authoring the premise is the human's role (premise / high-level direction); the AI authors the FACTS, the ERAS, the REGIONS, the CAUSAL CHAINS, and the PROSE (the ai-first-end-to-end doctrine).

firewall.orchestrator: this lineage (R570): writes manifest + premise + briefs + runbook; rebuilds the store FRESH from the author's JSON; runs the deterministic gates (PIN-1); runs the era-as-order scan (PIN-2), the past->future negative-control remove (PIN-3), and the cross-region/cross-character negative-control remove (PIN-4); runs the optional-fork per-era quest-graph derivation (PIN-5); selects the render slice; assembles the cross-age briefing; writes the extractor vocab; runs the render leak+fidelity gate over the blind re-extraction. NEVER authors a fact, renders a scene, re-extracts, or judges. Records whether the era/region/causation JOINs were painful enough to register a tooling pull (a report-era-graph / report-quest-graph-per-era use).
firewall.blind_author: ONE fresh-context subagent (authoring). Input = premise.md + author-brief.md ONLY. Authors the fact base + the two ages + the causal chains + the disclosure plan via mnemosyne-cli, using the gates as self-consistency feedback. BLIND to the hypothesis, the pins (incl. that era-as-order, structural causation, and cross-region causation are measured by remove-and-rescan), the substrate mapping, the rubric, the decision rule, and the render/judge stages.
firewall.blind_render: ONE fresh-context subagent (render). Input = render-brief.md + the trunk outline + ONE Withering-future outline ONLY. Renders warm played prose for that slice. BLIND to the pins, the rubric, the hypothesis, the original facts.json/plan, and that leak/fidelity/game-feel are tested.
firewall.blind_extractor: ONE fresh-context subagent. Input = extractor-brief.md + the rendered slice + vocab.md (entity/predicate/frame IDS + forks_at ONLY). Re-extracts facts from the PROSE, canon_from = the prose's own ## sc-NN markers, EXPLICIT only. BLIND to the original facts and the disclosure plan (a genuine blind comparison, the R512 method).
firewall.blind_judges: 3 fresh-context subagents. Input = judge-brief.md + the cross-age briefing + the rendered slice ONLY (no frame labels, no plan, no internal reports). BLIND to the experiment, the hypothesis, that the content is AI-authored, the pins, and each other.
firewall.contamination_bound: the R469 discipline (the 13th use of the blind-experiment family). The lineage that resolved the axis may not author, render, re-extract, or judge its instance.

PIN_1_authoring_clean.claim: PIN. The author CONVERGES to a gate-clean (store-consistent) two-age base within a bounded loop budget.
criteria_all_required: import of the final sections.json + facts.json into a fresh empty schema-23 seed = 0 errors (loads clean).

validate-continuity --order --rules: structural violations = 0 AND interval violations = 0 (includes evidence-reachability, off-branch, and the R449 exclusive-possession / R547 succession rules on every load-bearing world-state object — the cistern vent state and the ford state).
report-fork-tree --order: the single optional fork (plant-or-not) PLACED; 0 unplaced fork points; the fork has 2 child Withering futures; every world-line reaches a terminal.
report-playthrough-manuscript --world W --order, EVERY W: unplaced = 0, undecidable = 0.
report-timeline-gaps --world W --order, every W: 0 gap / 0 unreached scene.
report-payoff-coverage --order: computed; every dangling setup is one the author-log declares INTENTIONALLY open (e.g. the not-planted future's lost far-field), NOT an accidental hole.
PIN_1_authoring_clean.budget: bounded authoring loop; if the author cannot reach this, PIN-1 FAILS = the tooling-friction branch (record the iteration count + the stuck gate).

PIN_2_era_as_order_not_branch.claim: PIN (the headline — operationalizes the R570 substrate resolution as a falsifiable property of authored data). The two ages are an ORDER coordinate on ONE world-line, not two frames and not (for the canonical spine) two branches.
PIN_2_era_as_order_not_branch.definition: An ERA = a contiguous band of scenes in canon order (the Founding-Age scenes, then the Withering-Age scenes). A SHARED PLACE = an Entity referenced by both a Founding-Age fact and a Withering-Age fact. A STABLE PROP ACROSS ERAS = a DisclosureSurface `object` that resolves to a MapLocator in BOTH a Founding-Age scene and a Withering-Age scene.
criteria_all_required: there exist >= 2 shared-place Entities each referenced by at least one Founding-Age fact AND at least one Withering-Age fact, on the SAME world-line (the canonical spine world-line, MAIN_BRANCH or its pre-fork prefix).

for >= 1 of those places, the SAME disclosure-surface `object` resolves to a MapLocator in a Founding-Age scene AND in a Withering-Age scene (the same prop touched in both ages) — confirmed via report-playable-world / the coverage surface.

canon order places EVERY Founding-Age scene of the canonical spine STRICTLY BEFORE every Withering-Age scene of the same world-line (CanonOrder::le holds past->future, never the reverse) — the eras are an ORDER band, not interleaved and not a fork between them.

at least one Founding-Age fact is a setup (payoff_expectation=Expected) PAID OFF by a Withering-Age fact (R442 across time-distance), gate-clean; AND at least one Founding-Age world-state value SUPERSEDES into a Withering-Age value on the same object (R547 supersedes_in_frame + holds_at), gate-clean.

the eras are NOT modeled as two epistemic frames of one moment and NOT (for the canonical spine) as two sibling branches: the Founding and Withering facts of the spine share a world-line and differ only in canon-order position (and may differ in POV frame per character, which is the orthogonal R541 axis).
PIN_2_era_as_order_not_branch.honest_limit: PIN-2 tests the DECLARATIVE time STRUCTURE — that the two ages sit as an ordered band on one world-line with shared places and a real past->future link. It does NOT test a played, time-flowing simulation (the wend-pipe 'running' time) — that is PLAY navigation over the frozen substrate (R560), pinion's, not Mnemosyne's. 'The two ages are ordered and linked' is not 'time runs'. Stated per feedback_dont_downplay_experiment_flaws.

PIN_3_past_future_causation_is_gated.claim: PIN (the deterministic teeth — past->future causation is STRUCTURAL, not prose-only). Removing a Founding-Age setup makes its Withering-Age payoff surface as dangling.
PIN_3_past_future_causation_is_gated.definition: A NEGATIVE CONTROL: the orchestrator takes the gate-clean base, REMOVES one Founding-Age setup fact (e.g. Sera's cistern-seal), rebuilds fresh, and re-runs report-payoff-coverage + validate-continuity.
criteria_all_required: on the UNMODIFIED base: the chosen Founding-Age setup is paid off (not dangling) in the Withering-Age terminal world(s) — the causal link is present and gate-clean.

on the REMOVED base: the Withering-Age payoff fact now surfaces as dangling (an uncredited payoff) OR the world-state succession gate now flags the missing predecessor — i.e. the future EFFECT loses its past CAUSE structurally, caught by a gate, not merely unmentioned in prose.
the remove is SURGICAL (one fact) and otherwise gate-clean — the only new violation traces to the removed cause (no incidental breakage masking the result).
PIN_3_past_future_causation_is_gated.honest_limit: PIN-3 proves the link is GATE-ENFORCED (remove cause -> effect dangles). It does not prove the AUTHOR intended every nuance of the causation — only that the structural dependency the author encoded is real. The richer 'this specific cause is sufficient/necessary in-world' is the R476 commonsense ceiling, judged not gated.

PIN_4_multichar_crossregion_causation.claim: PIN (NEW, owner-added — the axis the owner explicitly asked to test). A multi-character cross-region cause-effect chain is STRUCTURAL: an act by character A in region X (Founding) is paid off by a fact by character B in region Y (Withering), and removing the source-region setup makes the other-region payoff dangle.
PIN_4_multichar_crossregion_causation.definition: The load-bearing Halden(Loom)->Ode(Fields) chain. >= 2 frames (characters) act in >= 2 distinct regions on the SAME world-line; the setup fact and the payoff fact are in DIFFERENT frames and DIFFERENT regions (and different eras). PIN-4 reuses the PIN-3 negative-control method on THIS chain.
criteria_all_required: >= 2 person-frames each carry at least one fact, and those facts are placed in >= 2 distinct region Entities (region X != region Y).

there exists a setup fact F_setup with frame = A, in region X, in the Founding Age (payoff_expectation=Expected), PAID OFF by a fact F_payoff with frame = B (B != A), in region Y (Y != X), in the Withering Age — cross-era AND cross-region AND cross-frame, gate-clean (R442 cross-frame payoff, which 'crosses FRAMES freely … but never worlds').
NEGATIVE CONTROL: remove F_setup (region X, frame A), rebuild fresh -> F_payoff (region Y, frame B) surfaces as dangling — the cross-region cross-character causal link is STRUCTURAL, not prose-only.
the chain is on ONE world-line (the same playthrough world), never crossing worlds (consistent with the R442 'never cross worlds' invariant).
PIN_4_multichar_crossregion_causation.honest_scope: this tests TIME-LAGGED cross-region causation (a different region => usually a time gap => a partial-order chain, which IS expressible). Genuine SIMULTANEOUS state-COMBINATION (an AND-join: two characters acting in the SAME instant on the SAME thing, states merging) is OUT of scope — that is the R549-confirmed UNBUILT L3 (interfering AND-threads), pull-gated. Named per feedback_dont_downplay_experiment_flaws.

PIN_5_optional_fork_per_era_state.claim: PIN (optional acts split the future, and per-era state derives exactly). The plant-or-not optional Founding act forks two Withering futures, and report-quest-graph (R568/R569) derives the per-era / per-world state correctly.
criteria_all_required: the plant-or-not choice is the single fork (report-fork-tree): 2 child Withering futures, each terminal.

the 'far field reachable' obligation is DISCHARGED in the planted future and OPEN in the not-planted future (per-world divergence — the R559/R568 'state DERIVED per world-line' claim demonstrated on the time axis).

report-quest-graph --order (if a kind=quest entity is authored for the cross-age objective) derives per-world Done/Open exactly matching the payoff-coverage divergence; if the author models the objective WITHOUT a kind=quest entity, this criterion is satisfied by report-payoff-coverage per-world divergence alone (report-quest-graph is the projection, not a requirement to author quests).
PIN_5_optional_fork_per_era_state.honest_limit: PIN-5 is the one OPTIONAL pin — if the author renders the optional act as a non-forking flavour choice, PIN-5 is recorded N/A (not a failure), and the canonical spine (PIN-2..PIN-4) carries the result. The fork is the cleanest demonstration but not load-bearing for the core hypothesis.

render_acceptance_gate.note: NOT a renumbered pin (preserves the owner's PIN-1..PIN-5 set) — a render-slice leak+fidelity gate feeding the judges, the dnd-quest PIN-4 / R515 warm-render-then-gate discipline reused.
render_acceptance_gate.leak: validate-disclosure-leak --telling wend --against <reextracted> --world <rendered-future> --truth-frame gt = 0 premature leaks of the withheld Founding-Age CAUSE before its first_at (the bearer's cross scene) on that road, AND vocab_shared > 0 (the R510 F5 non-vacuous guard), exit 0.
render_acceptance_gate.fidelity: validate-render-fidelity over the re-extraction vs order.json, the rendered future = off_path 0, unplaced 0, reached_terminal true.
render_acceptance_gate.repair_protocol: if a gate flags, that is the render-then-gate-REPAIR signal (R515): record the violation, the scene-scoped repair, and the repair COUNT as the posture tax. Repairs stay in the warm register; do NOT silently fix.
render_acceptance_gate.scope_note: the render is a SLICE (Founding trunk + one Withering future) — the structural pins cover all world-lines; the render covers one future's prose so the judges read warm time-travel prose with a real reveal.

judged_no_pin.procedure: 3 fresh blind judges, judge-brief.md rubric, over (1) the orchestrator-assembled CROSS-AGE BRIEFING (each region in both ages; each causal chain as early-act -> late-consequence with where/who/when; the optional fork's two futures; assembled from report-payoff-coverage + report-playable-world + the entity/claim JSON) and (2) the rendered slice (run/render/*.md, no frame labels, no plan, no internal reports). Single arm (a capability test, no A/B).
judged_no_pin.axes: era-consistency (the two ages of each place cohere) / causal-payoff-satisfaction (early acts pay off satisfyingly in the late age) / cross-region-coherence (distant places line up in time — Loom-act reaching Fields-consequence reads as coherent) / time-travel-legibility (the reader can hold the two ages + the reveal) / overall-time-travel-game (1-5 each, scene-cited) + two forced calls (what most makes it feel like time travel / what most undercuts it; reads-as-a-real-time-travel-game vs eras-stapled-together).
judged_no_pin.control_crossref: FREE settled signals (orchestrator computes, judges blind): R545 (depth+breadth coherence 5/5); R551 (warm render leak=0); R562/R564 (the dnd-quest fork+map read as a real game). Those settled coherence/warmth/quest-feel; the open question here is whether the TIME axis + cross-region causation reads as a coherent time-travel game.
judged_no_pin.outcome_pin: NONE. The procedure is pinned; the time-travel-game verdict is the open question.

decision_rule_pre_committed.all_pins_hold_and_judged_good: PIN-1..PIN-4 hold (PIN-5 holds or N/A) AND judges find (majority >= 4): era-consistency AND causal-payoff-satisfaction AND overall-time-travel-game, with no major era/causation break => an AI self-authored a real TIME-AXIS game on ZERO new substrate; the R570 resolution (era = order coordinate, cross-era/cross-region causation = R442+R547) is CONFIRMED on self-authored data and READS as time travel. The on-axis expected outcome; its value = the time axis proven end-to-end + the multi-character cross-region causal chain shown structural.
decision_rule_pre_committed.pin2_fails: the author converges (PIN-1) but collapses the two ages into frames or unrelated branches, or fails to order past<future, or fails to share a place across ages => the era-as-order model is harder to AUTHOR than the resolution assumed (the mapping is correct but the authoring affordance is missing) => diagnose; record as a measured pull (an era-authoring helper, or a report-era-band projection). NOT a refutation of the ontology; a finding about authorability.
decision_rule_pre_committed.pin3_or_pin4_fails: a causal link the author encoded in prose does NOT survive the remove-and-rescan (the future effect does NOT dangle when the past cause is removed) => the causation was PROSE-ONLY, not structural (the scale-floor store-consistency != causal-coherence residue, surfaced by the negative control on the TIME axis) => a high-value honest finding: the author asserted a time-cause the gates do not enforce; diagnose whether the substrate COULD have enforced it (a missing setup/payoff or succession typing the author skipped) vs a genuine gap.
decision_rule_pre_committed.pin5_na_or_fails: if the optional act does not fork (N/A) the result rests on the canonical spine; if it forks but per-era state does not derive correctly => a real report-quest-graph / payoff-coverage divergence bug on the time axis => a substrate finding worth a fix round.
decision_rule_pre_committed.render_gate_fails: the warm render leaks the withheld Founding cause early or drifts off the future road => record the repair count as the posture tax for a time-travel reveal slice; if repairs cannot clear it, the warm discipline costs more when the reveal is a time-cross.
decision_rule_pre_committed.judged_time_travel_low: all pins hold but judged era-consistency or causal-payoff or overall < 4 majority => the structure is correct but does NOT READ as time travel (the structure-consistency != game-feel residue) => a craft ceiling (R476), blind-judged, not a substrate gap; a high-value honest finding about the gap between a correct time graph and a time-travel game that feels coherent.
decision_rule_pre_committed.tooling_pull: ORTHOGONAL: the orchestrator records whether assembling PIN-2..PIN-5 + the cross-age briefing from separate reports (payoff-coverage + playable-world + the entity/claim JSON + the per-era band scan) was painful enough to justify a report-era-graph projection or promoting report-quest-graph to per-era. This experiment is the consumer whose pull (or lack of it) decides whether such a projection is built next — the R556->R557 design-then-build-when-pulled discipline.

grading_procedure.step_0: orchestrator: this manifest sha256-pinned in the R570 ledger, committed pre-execution (R570 = the manifest round). GOTCHA: report-playable-world / report-quest-graph require a current CLI install; a pre-R568 binary lacks report-quest-graph (the binary-skew lesson) — execution must `cargo install --path crates/mnemosyne-cli --force` (and restart mnemosyne-mcp) first.
grading_procedure.step_1_author: spawn the blind author (premise.md + author-brief.md) -> run/author/ (sections/facts/order/narrative-rules JSON + disclosure plan + gate-clean store + author-log.md).
grading_procedure.step_2_pins: orchestrator rebuilds the store FRESH from the author's JSON; runs PIN-1 gate criteria; runs the PIN-2 era-as-order scan (shared-place Entities x per-era scenes x same MapLocator object across ages x canon-order past<future x >=1 R442 cross-time payoff + >=1 R547 succession); runs the PIN-3 + PIN-4 negative-control removes (remove the Founding setup, rebuild fresh, confirm the Withering payoff dangles); runs the PIN-5 optional-fork per-era derivation. Records every verdict verbatim + the JOIN painfulness note.
grading_procedure.step_3_slice_and_briefing: orchestrator selects the render future (the planted future — the richer one, most cross-age payoffs + the withheld-cause reveal); assembles the Founding trunk outline + that future's outline (report-playthrough-manuscript --world W --telling wend --order) to run/manuscripts/; assembles the cross-age briefing to run/briefing/cross-age-briefing.md.
grading_procedure.step_4_render: spawn the blind render (render-brief.md + the two outlines) -> run/render/world-*.md + render-log.md.
grading_procedure.step_5_extract: orchestrator writes run/extract/vocab.md (ids + forks_at only); spawn the blind extractor (extractor-brief.md + the rendered slice + vocab.md) -> run/extract/reextracted.atomic.json + extract-log.md.
grading_procedure.step_6_render_gate: orchestrator runs the render leak + fidelity gate over the re-extraction, records verbatim (incl. any repair count).
grading_procedure.step_7_judges: spawn 3 blind judges (judge-brief.md + the cross-age briefing + the rendered slice) -> run/judges/judge-{1,2,3}.md.
grading_procedure.step_8_decide: orchestrator applies the pre-committed decision rule, writes report.md, appends the R571 changelog entry + commit. Do NOT push (the push consent gate).
grading_procedure.no_new_harness_code: reuse mnemosyne-cli verbs verbatim; PIN-2..PIN-5 + the briefing are JSON/report scans + negative-control removes recorded in report.md; no tools/ or crate change. Scratch *.atomic.json / *.playthrough.json stay gitignored; tracked evidence per runbook.md.

predictions_preregistered.PRED_1_authoring: PIN-1 likely HOLDS at this scale (~45-55 scenes split across two ages; tractable, R524/R541/R545/R562 precedent). The two-age structure is MORE authoring surface than a single-era game (every place authored twice, the succession typed) — some extra loop iterations plausible.
predictions_preregistered.PRED_2_era_as_order: PIN-2 is the GENUINE open question — first time a blind author writes two ages of one place. Honest expectation: PLAUSIBLE that a blind author orders past<future and shares places (the premise hands them the structure), but NOT assured — a blind author could collapse the ages into two frames ('the same moment, two beliefs') or two unrelated branches, which would FAIL era-as-order. The remove-and-rescan negative control is what makes the causation claim falsifiable rather than self-reported.
predictions_preregistered.PRED_3_causation_structural: PIN-3/PIN-4 are the sharpest test. Honest expectation: the canonical cistern chain (PIN-3) likely survives the remove (a clean setup/payoff). The cross-region cross-character chain (PIN-4) is the RISK — a blind author may render Halden->Ode as PROSE coherence ('the stone was old') without a typed setup/payoff edge, in which case the remove does NOT dangle and PIN-4 reveals prose-only causation (the store-consistency != causal-coherence residue on the time axis). That failure would be a high-value finding, not downplayed.
predictions_preregistered.PRED_4_render: the render gate likely HOLDS (R515/R551/R562 warm render gate-clean). Some repair count > 0 plausible — a time-travel reveal is exactly where a renderer is tempted to blurt the withheld Founding cause in a late-age scene before the cross.
predictions_preregistered.PRED_5_fork: PIN-5 likely HOLDS if the author forks the plant-or-not act (the premise asks for it); N/A if they render it as flavour. Per-era divergence is the R559/R568 mechanism, proven.
predictions_preregistered.PRED_game_feel: NO pin (the open question). Honest expectation: era-consistency and causal-payoff likely read OK from a clean structure; the real risk is the two ages reading as STAPLED TOGETHER (a Founding half then a Withering half) rather than a place the reader feels persisting THROUGH time, and the cross-region chain (Loom->Fields, generations apart) reading as a coincidence rather than a consequence. Time-travel game-feel is the most likely place this finds a real limit.
predictions_preregistered.PRED_pull: the era/region/causation hand-JOIN is predicted MODERATELY painful — composing per-era / per-region causal state from payoff-coverage + playable-world + the entity JSON + a per-era band scan by hand is exactly the JOIN a report-era-graph (or per-era report-quest-graph) would own. Enough to register a real pull, not necessarily enough to justify a build (the R556->R557 discipline).

honest_caveats: n=1 premise / 1 author / 1 render / 1 extractor / 3 judges — one instance, not a distribution (the standing single-instance caveat of this experiment family).

the experiment tests the DECLARATIVE time STRUCTURE (two ordered ages, shared places, gate-enforced past->future links), NOT a played time-flowing simulation — the wend-pipe 'running' time is PLAY navigation over a frozen substrate (R560), pinion's, not Mnemosyne's. We show the two ages are ordered, linked, and the cross-region cause is structural; we do NOT show 'time runs'. Named, not downplayed (feedback_dont_downplay_experiment_flaws).

the SIMULTANEOUS state-COMBINATION case (an AND-join: two characters acting in the same instant on the same thing) is OUT of scope — that is the R549-confirmed UNBUILT L3 (interfering AND-threads), pull-gated. This experiment covers TIME-LAGGED cross-region causation (a partial-order chain), which is expressible; it does not cover concurrent merging state.

PIN-2's era-band definition (Founding scenes strictly before Withering scenes on a world-line) and PIN-4's cross-region floor (>=2 frames in >=2 regions, one cross-frame cross-region payoff) are defensible floors, not derived constants — pinned pre-execution so they cannot be retrofitted, but a different reasonable floor could flip a marginal verdict.

the render is a SLICE (Founding trunk + one Withering future), so the render gate + game-feel see ONE future's prose; the structural pins cover all world-lines, but the judged game-feel of the not-planted future is inferred from the briefing, not read as prose.

the negative-control removes (PIN-3/PIN-4) prove the link is GATE-ENFORCED, not that the author's causation is the BEST or only model — a future effect dangling when its encoded past cause is removed proves structural dependency, not in-world sufficiency (the R476 commonsense ceiling, judged not gated).

binary-skew gotcha: report-playable-world (R557) + report-quest-graph (R568) require a current CLI install; a pre-R568 binary lacks the verb (the reference_mnemosyne_mcp_binary_skew lesson). Execution reinstalls first.
